reasoning: I am walking into this booth furious at Mamata's people — the SSC scam is real, and those young Matua families deserve better than a system that treats them as vote-blocks. Biswajit Das is a defector who belongs nowhere, and TMC's administration is sloppy and corrupt. But I have spent five years watching what happens when I let local anger override my understanding of the larger stakes. The SSC jobs should never have been sold; that is TMC's rot. But the agencies raiding TMC ministers on election schedules, the temple consecration timed to maximum electoral effect, the systematic colonization of institutions — that is the deeper game, and it will shape how we live in Bengal for a decade. I will not hand BJP a supermajority because I am furious at Mamata's goons. My anger at TMC is legitimate and permanent. But I learned in 2019 that voting against a stronger BJP shift is my real priority. Shantanu Thakur promised the Matuas the world and delivered chaos; now he returns asking for continuity. Neither party sees us as citizens. But one will eventually lose an election; the other is building something meant to last.
primary_drivers: Fear of BJP institutional capture and ideological consolidation over time
Unwillingness to give BJP a supermajority despite legitimate anger at TMC corruption
Belief that local thuggishness can be voted out; ideological capture cannot
